_description: Mock chunk structures for CFR Title 21 Chapter III RAG pipeline. Designed for both dense vector retrieval and lexical graph traversal.
_chunking_strategy: paragraph-level with full hierarchy breadcrumbs. Each lettered paragraph (a), (b)... is its own chunk. Numbered sub-points (1), (2)... are embedded within the parent paragraph chunk unless the paragraph exceeds ~400 tokens, in which case each numbered sub-point becomes its own chunk with overlap_context from the parent. Roman-numeral points (i), (ii)... are always embedded within their numbered parent chunk. This keeps metric values together with their regulatory context and enables precise graph node creation.

chunks._comment: EXAMPLE 1 — Simple single-paragraph section. Short sections with one P element become a single 'section' chunk.

chapter.name: Office of National Drug Control Policy

subpart.name: Freedom of Information Act Policies and Procedures

chunks.text: The purpose of this part is to prescribe rules, guidelines and procedures to implement the Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), as amended, 5 U.S.C. 552.

chunks._comment: EXAMPLE 2 — Multi-paragraph section, one chunk per top-level lettered paragraph. This is § 1401.11(a). Metric values are preserved intact within their regulatory context. The section_preamble carries the governing intro sentence so each paragraph chunk is self-contained.

chapter.name: Office of National Drug Control Policy

subpart.name: Freedom of Information Act Policies and Procedures

chunks.section_preamble: ONDCP will assess a fee to process FOIA requests in accordance with the provisions of this section and Uniform Freedom of Information Fee Schedule and Guidelines published by the Office of Management and Budget (OMB Guidelines). ONDCP shall ensure that searches, review and duplication are conducted in the most efficient and the least expensive manner. ONDCP will ordinarily collect all applicable fees before sending copies of records to a requester. ONDCP will charge the following fees unless a waiver or reduction of fees is granted under § 1401.15, or the total fee to be charged is less than $25.00.
chunks.text: (a) Search for records. ONDCP will charge $77.00 per hour, which is a blended hourly rate for all personnel that respond to FOIA requests plus 16 percent of that rate to cover benefits.

metrics.context: blended hourly rate for FOIA search
metrics.modifier: plus 16 percent of that rate to cover benefits

metrics.context: minimum fee threshold below which no fee is charged (from preamble)

chunks._comment: EXAMPLE 3 — Paragraph with multiple metrics. § 1401.11(c) duplication fees: all sub-rules are under 400 tokens and tightly coupled, so numbered sub-points are embedded within this single paragraph chunk. Splitting them would orphan metric values from their conditions.

chapter.name: Office of National Drug Control Policy

subpart.name: Freedom of Information Act Policies and Procedures

chunks.section_preamble: ONDCP will assess a fee to process FOIA requests in accordance with the provisions of this section and Uniform Freedom of Information Fee Schedule and Guidelines published by the Office of Management and Budget (OMB Guidelines).
chunks.text: (c) Duplication of records. We will charge duplication fees to all requesters. We will honor your preference for receiving a record in a particular format if we can readily reproduce it in the form or format requested. If we provide photocopies, we will make one copy per request at the cost of $.10 per page. For copies of records produced on tapes, disks or other media, we will charge the direct costs of producing the copy, including operator time. Where we must scan paper documents in order to comply with your preference to receive the records in an electronic format, we will charge you the direct costs associated with scanning those materials. For other forms of duplication, we will charge the direct costs. We will provide the first 100 pages of duplication (or the cost equivalent for other media) without charge except for requesters seeking records for a commercial use.

chunks._comment: EXAMPLE 4 — Subparagraph-level chunk. When a numbered sub-point under a lettered paragraph is long enough to stand alone (>100 tokens) it becomes its own chunk. overlap_context carries the parent paragraph label and sibling listing so the embedding is not context-free.

chapter.name: Office of National Drug Control Policy

subpart.name: Freedom of Information Act Policies and Procedures

chunks.section_preamble: Requests for records that are in ONDCP's custody but in which other agencies have equities shall be reviewed by ONDCP and then ONDCP will either consult with or refer the records to the other agency or agencies for further processing.
chunks.text: (b) Referral—(1) Determination. When ONDCP believes that a different agency or component is best able to determine whether to disclose the record, ONDCP will refer the responsibility for responding to the request regarding that record to that agency. Ordinarily, the agency that originated the record is presumed to be the best agency to make the disclosure determination. However, if the agency processing the request and the originating agency jointly agree that the agency processing the request is in the best position to respond regarding the record, then the record may be handled as a consultation.

chunks._comment: EXAMPLE 5 — Definition chunk. Each defined term in a Definitions section gets its own chunk of type 'definition'. These become high-connectivity nodes in the lexical graph, with DEFINES_TERM edges out and USES_TERM edges in from every chunk that references the term.

chapter.name: Office of National Drug Control Policy

subpart.name: Freedom of Information Act Policies and Procedures

chunks.section_preamble: For the purpose of this part, all the terms defined in the Freedom of Information Act apply.
chunks.text: Direct costs means the expense actually expended to search, review, or duplicate in response to a FOIA request. For example, direct costs include 116% of the salary of the employee performing work (i.e., the basic rate of pay for the employee plus 16 percent of that rate to cover benefits) and the actual costs incurred while operating equipment.

metrics.context: salary multiplier for direct cost calculation
metrics.modifier: base rate of pay plus 16% benefits

chunks._comment: EXAMPLE 6a — Overflow chunk part 1 of 2. When a paragraph exceeds ~400 tokens it is split at a sentence boundary. This chunk is part 1. No overlap_context needed for the first part.

chapter.name: Office of National Drug Control Policy

subpart.name: Freedom of Information Act Policies and Procedures

chunks.text: (c) Adverse determinations. If ONDCP makes an adverse determination denying a request in any respect, it must notify the requester of that determination in writing. Adverse determinations, or denials of requests, include decisions that: The requested record is exempt, in whole or in part; the request does not reasonably describe the records sought; the information requested is not a record subject to the FOIA; the requested record does not exist, cannot be located, or has been destroyed; or the requested record is not readily reproducible in the form or format sought by the requester.

chunks._comment: EXAMPLE 6b — Overflow chunk part 2 of 2. overlap_context.overlap_tail_text contains ~50 tokens from the tail of part 1, prepended during embedding to preserve metric-condition continuity across the split boundary.

chapter.name: Office of National Drug Control Policy

subpart.name: Freedom of Information Act Policies and Procedures

chunks.text: Adverse determinations also include denials involving fees or fee waiver matters or denials of requests for expedited processing.

overlap_context.overlap_tail_text: or the requested record is not readily reproducible in the form or format sought by the requester.

lexical_graph_schema._description: Node and edge types for the knowledge graph layer. chunk_id values serve as node IDs for CHUNK nodes. Hierarchy nodes (TITLE, CHAPTER, PART, SUBPART, SECTION) form a containment tree. Leaf edges (CROSS_REFERENCES, DEFINES_TERM, USES_TERM, CONTAINS_METRIC, CITES) enable multi-hop traversal for complex regulatory queries.

example.name: Office of National Drug Control Policy
Freedom of Information Act Policies and Procedures

node_types.note: Leaf-level RAG retrieval unit. Embedding vector stored separately in vector DB.

edge_types.label: USES_TERM
edge_types.note: for all chunks where term appears in text

edge_types.label: CONTAINS_METRIC
edge_types.note: one edge per entry in metrics[]

edge_types.label: CITES
edge_types.note: one edge per entry in citations[]

edge_types.label: OVERFLOW_CONTINUES
edge_types.note: links part N to part N+1 for overflow chunks

chunking_strategy_rationale.chosen_approach: paragraph-level (one chunk per lettered paragraph (a)/(b)/...), with definition-level splitting for Definitions sections and sentence-boundary overflow splitting for paragraphs >400 tokens
chunking_strategy_rationale.why_not_full_section_chunks: Sections like §1401.11 contain 4 independent paragraphs each with distinct metric values and conditions. A single section chunk forces the retriever to return all fee rules even when only one is relevant, reducing precision and bloating LLM context.
chunking_strategy_rationale.why_not_sub_point_only_chunks: Sub-points like the duplication rules in §1401.11(c) list conditions that all apply together to the same fee type. Splitting each sentence produces chunks with orphaned metric values (e.g. '$0.10 per page') divorced from their governing conditions, which misleads the LLM during generation.
chunking_strategy_rationale.why_paragraph_level_is_optimal: A lettered paragraph (a), (b)... is the smallest self-contained regulatory obligation in CFR structure. Each one answers: who does what, under what condition, with what threshold. Metrics always appear within the paragraph that defines the condition triggering them. Typical size is 50–250 tokens — tight enough for focused retrieval, rich enough for self-contained context.
chunking_strategy_rationale.overflow_strategy: Paragraphs exceeding 400 tokens are split at sentence boundaries. Each continuation chunk prepends a ~50-token overlap tail from its predecessor. This preserves metric-condition proximity across the split boundary without duplicating large blocks of text.
chunking_strategy_rationale.section_preamble_field: Multi-paragraph sections often have a governing introductory sentence before the (a)/(b) enumeration (e.g. 'ONDCP will charge the following fees unless...'). This preamble is stored in every paragraph-level chunk from that section so that retrieval of any individual paragraph always surfaces the governing condition.
chunking_strategy_rationale.metric_handling: All numeric values with units are extracted into the structured metrics[] array in addition to remaining verbatim in the text field. This enables direct graph queries like 'what sections mention a threshold of $25' without full-text scan, and allows the graph to connect METRIC nodes across chunks that share the same threshold value.
chunking_strategy_rationale.definition_splitting: Every defined term in a Definitions section gets its own DEFINITION chunk. These become high-fan-out nodes in the lexical graph: one DEFINES_TERM edge out, many USES_TERM edges in from every chunk where the term appears. This enables the retriever to return the definition alongside any regulatory obligation that depends on it.
chunking_strategy_rationale.graph_rag_benefit: The CROSS_REFERENCES edge directly connects §1401.11 fee paragraph chunks to §1401.15 waiver chunks. A query about fee waivers triggers graph traversal that returns both the waiver conditions and the base fee rules in a single hop, without relying solely on embedding similarity — which might miss the relationship if the embedding spaces diverge.

chunk_type_codes.para-{label}-sub-{n}: numbered subparagraph split out, e.g. para-b-sub-1
